input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom input bar on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. Let me provide guidance based on what I know:

In .NET 9 and earlier, you could use `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` to make the layout resize when the keyboard opens. However, in Android 11+ (API 30+), `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` was deprecated. .NET 10 (which targets newer Android APIs) likely respects this deprecation.


The modern approach in .NET 10 is to use `WindowInsetsCompat` to handle keyboard (IME) insets directly, which is the replacement for the deprecated resize behavior. You can either configure this through AndroidX.Core edge-to-edge APIs or set `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` in the AndroidManifest.xml while properly handling the inset callbacks.

For .NET MAUI specifically, the practical solutions involve using the newer `KeyboardAutoManager` (introduced in .NET 9) which automatically handles keyboard avoidance, or manually handling `WindowInsets` on Android. For a chat screen with input at the bottom, setting `adjustResize` in the manifest combined with proper inset handling is the most reliable approach, though if that doesn't work as expected, `adjustPan` can be used as an alternative.

Since .NET 10 now defaults to edge-to-edge layouts following Android 15 requirements, the keyboard handling has shifted to rely on inset-based adjustments rather than simple resizing. The practical solution is to keep `adjustResize` in the manifest while using platform-specific code to listen for keyboard insets and apply appropriate padding to the layout, or more simply, wrapping the content in a `ScrollView` to let the framework handle the adjustment automatically.
